title: callitriche cophocarpa
intro: Callitriche cophocarpa, also known as creeping winter-surviving water-starwort, is a type of aquatic or semi-aquatic plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Callitriche cophocarpa include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Callitriche cophocarpa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
